rubric.taskPrompt: Write a comment in the role of an employee representative at NorthStar Logistics AG. Take a clear position on introducing AI tools at the workplace. Follow this structure: introduction, three counter-arguments (strongest to weakest), a bridge, three own arguments (weakest to strongest), and a conclusion. Each argument must contain a claim, a reason, and an example.
expectedPoints: Introduction names the topic and the writer's role and states a clear position
Counter-argument 1 (strongest) with claim, reason and example
Counter-argument 2 with claim, reason and example
Counter-argument 3 (weakest) with claim, reason and example
Bridge that moves from the counter-arguments to the own position
Own argument 1 (weakest) with claim, reason and example
Own argument 2 with claim, reason and example
Own argument 3 (strongest) with claim, reason and example
Conclusion that restates the position and gives an outlook

criteria.name: Structure and argumentation
criteria.description: Follows the required structure (intro, three counter-arguments strongest to weakest, bridge, three own arguments weakest to strongest, conclusion) as flowing prose.

levels.label: very good
levels.descriptor: All parts present and ordered as required, arguments developed as connected prose.

levels.label: good to satisfactory
levels.descriptor: Most parts present and mostly in order, some arguments carried further than others.

levels.label: developing
levels.descriptor: Several structural parts still to be added or reordered.

criteria.name: Argument completeness (CRE)
criteria.description: Each of the six arguments is only counted when claim, reason and example are all present. This criterion checks that all-or-nothing rule across arguments.

allOrNothing.rule: An argument only counts with claim AND reason AND example.

criteria.name: Sentence structure
criteria.description: Variety, complexity and cohesion of sentences; flowing prose rather than labels.

criteria.name: Vocabulary
criteria.description: Range and precision of everyday vocabulary, own wording.

criteria.name: Topic vocabulary and connectives
criteria.description: Topic vocabulary about work and technology plus connectives that link arguments.

calibrationSamples.text: As an employee representative at NorthStar Logistics AG, I would like to comment on the plan to introduce AI tools at our workplace. Many colleagues worry about their jobs. They fear that routine tasks will be automated, for example the sorting of delivery notes, which three colleagues do every morning. Another concern is data protection, because the tools collect a lot of information about how we work. A third worry is that people who are not used to computers could be left behind. However, there are also strong reasons in favour of the change. First, the tools can take over tiring repetitive steps, so we have more time for customer calls. Second, they can reduce mistakes in the routing of parcels, which currently cost us extra hours. Most importantly, if we help to shape the rules for these tools, we can protect our colleagues and still profit from the new possibilities. In conclusion, I support a careful introduction with clear rules and training for everyone.
calibrationSamples.note: Complete structure, CRE met in every argument, confident language.

calibrationSamples.text: I am an employee representative. I want to talk about AI at work. Some colleagues do not like the idea. Jobs could be automated. Data protection is also a topic. And some people are afraid of computers. On the other hand AI can help. It saves time. It can reduce errors. It can make the work easier. In conclusion I think we should try it with rules.
calibrationSamples.note: Structure recognisable, but arguments without examples, so CRE is 0.
